things.name: Sumela Monastery
things.description: Extraordinary Greek Orthodox monastery clinging to a sheer cliff face 1,200 metres above the Altindere Valley; founded in 386 AD and one of the most dramatic sites in Turkey

things.name: Hagia Sophia of Trabzon
things.description: 13th-century Byzantine church built by the Empire of Trebizond; exquisite frescoes and a seaside hilltop location make this one of the finest Byzantine buildings outside Istanbul

things.name: Trabzon Museum
things.description: Set in a beautiful early 20th-century villa; collections cover the ancient Pontic kingdom, Byzantine Empire of Trebizond, Ottoman era and ethnographic treasures of the Black Sea region

things.name: Ataturk Mansion
things.description: Elegant early 20th-century mansion where Mustafa Kemal Ataturk stayed in 1924; beautifully restored with original furnishings and panoramic views over the Black Sea coast

things.name: Trabzon Bazaar and Covered Market
things.description: Historic Ottoman bazaar district with the Bedesten (covered market) from the 14th century; famous for local hazelnuts, anchovies, cornbread and handwoven textiles

things.name: Uzungol Lake
things.description: Breathtakingly beautiful mountain lake set among forested peaks 100km from Trabzon; one of Turkey's most photographed natural landscapes with traditional wooden houses

things.name: Trabzon Castle (Tabakhane Bridge)
things.description: Byzantine-era city castle and ancient Tabakhane Bridge spanning a dramatic ravine; the historic heart of Trebizond with walls dating from the 4th century

things.name: Black Sea Coast
things.description: Trabzon's dramatic Black Sea coastline with rocky beaches, anchovy fishing boats and seafront promenade; the Black Sea is central to Trabzon's identity and cuisine

things.name: Zeytinlik Monastery (Kizlar Manastiri)
things.description: Early Byzantine monastery ruins above Trabzon with panoramic views; one of several ancient religious sites that testify to the city's long Christian heritage

things.name: Medical Park Stadium (Papara Park)
things.description: Modern 40,700-seat stadium opened in 2016; home of Trabzonspor, Turkey's most successful Black Sea club with six league titles and UEFA Champions League participation
